title: 补齐 SBOM 完整性与锁定语义
outOfScope: Changing evidence source identity covered by R2
Installer evidence boundary covered by R4

riskAssessment.reason: Incomplete component discovery can create false confidence in dependency coverage.

executionDecision.reason: Contract is complete for the SBOM completeness audit and implementation.

preReviewWarnings: Do not claim transitive dependency coverage from a direct lockfile parser alone.
Verify every workflow uses occurrence and preserve lock hash semantics.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: All workflow Action uses occurrences and all locked direct dependencies are discovered in a repeatable SBOM report.
Transitive dependency coverage and lock hash guarantees are explicitly reported without overclaiming.
Regression tests cover Action discovery, direct dependency discovery, and incomplete-lock behavior.
Documentation states the real scope of --require-hashes and SBOM coverage.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The current SBOM parser discovers direct lock entries and workflow actions but does not make transitive dependency coverage or lock guarantees explicit.
constraints: Do not change R2 source identity semantics.
Keep generation deterministic and usable in offline CI.
intent.rationale: A report that distinguishes direct, transitive, and workflow coverage prevents supply-chain evidence from being interpreted more strongly than its inputs justify.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Every workflow uses occurrence is represented in the Action component inventory.
Every direct locked dependency is represented and lock hash semantics are reported.
Transitive dependency coverage is either generated from a reproducible environment or explicitly marked as unavailable.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized continuous R3-R11 remediation execution.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
